outcomes.leaf: Architect: floor max_files above 1 when tests are required

findings.summary: The floor-to-3 logic in `architect.py` applies to ALL leaves where `requires_tests=True` and `max_files < 3`, with no check on `execution_mode`. The test `test_requires_tests_floors_max_files_to_3` asserts that a Manual leaf with `requires_tests=True` and `max_files=1` retains `max_files=1`, but the code will force it to 3. Either the test will fail (regression), or there is hidden logic not visible in the diff — either way, the implementation and test are inconsistent and one of them is wrong.

The test never asserts the final `max_files` of the `"no tests"` Auto-OK leaf. Since Auto-OK leaves unconditionally have `requires_tests` set to `True` in `_enrich_auto_leaf`, a leaf supplied with `requires_tests=False` and `execution_mode="Auto-OK"` will have `requires_tests` overwritten and `max_files` floored to 3. The missing assertion silently hides this behaviour, giving false confidence that `requires_tests=False` Auto-OK leaves are exempt from the floor.

The forced floor of `max_files` to 3 is more aggressive than justified: many real tasks legitimately touch only 2 files (implementation + test), so Auto-OK leaves that truly need only 2 files will be given an inflated budget, potentially allowing workers extra slack and masking over-engineering.
